sections.title: Definition of the product
sections.text:
Peanuts, either in the pod or in the form of kernels, are obtained from varieties of the species Arachis hypogaea L.

a) those affected by freezing injury causing hard, translucent or discoloured flesh;
b) shrivelled kernels which are imperfectly developed and shrunken; and/or
c) those damaged by insects, worm cuts;
d) mechanical damage;
e) germinated kernels.
1% m/m To be determined

2%m/m

sections.title: Quality factors – general
sections.text:
Peanuts shall be safe and suitable for processing for human consumption.  
Peanuts shall be free from abnormal flavours, odours, living insects and mites.

sections.title: Mouldy, rancid or decayed kernels  0.2% m/m max
path_titles: Mouldy, rancid or decayed kernels  0.2% m/m max
sections.text:
– Mouldy kernels are defined as kernels with mould filaments visible to the naked eye.  
– Decayed kernels are defined as those showing visibly significant decomposition. 
– Rancid kernels are defined as those which have undergone oxidation of lipids (should not exceed 
5 meq active oxygen/kg) or the production of free fatty acids (should not exceed 1.0%) resulting in 
the production of disagreeable flavours.

sections.title: PACKAGING
sections.text:
6.1 Peanuts shall be packaged in such manner which will safeguard the hygienic, nutritional, technological, and organoleptic qualities of the product. Packaging will be sound, clean, dry, and free from insect infestation or fungal contamination.
6.2 Packing material shall be made of substances which are safe and suitable for their intended use, including new clean jute bags, tinplate containers, plastic or paper boxes or bags. They should not impart any toxic substance or undesirable odour or flavour to the product.
6.3 When the product is packaged in sacks, these must be clean, sturdy, and strongly sewn or sealed.

6.1 Peanuts shall be packaged in such manner which will safeguard the hygienic, nutritional, technological, and organoleptic qualities of the product. Packaging will be sound, clean, dry, and free from insect infestation or fungal contamination.
6.2 Packing material shall be made of substances which are safe and suitable for their intended use, including new clean jute bags, tinplate containers, plastic or paper boxes or bags. They should not impart any toxic substance or undesirable odour or flavour to the product.
6.3 When the product is packaged in sacks, these must be clean, sturdy, and strongly sewn or sealed.

page_spans.text:
6.1 Peanuts shall be packaged in such manner which will safeguard the hygienic, nutritional, technological, and organoleptic qualities of the product. Packaging will be sound, clean, dry, and free from insect infestation or fungal contamination.
6.2 Packing material shall be made of substances which are safe and suitable for their intended use, including new clean jute bags, tinplate containers, plastic or paper boxes or bags. They should not impart any toxic substance or undesirable odour or flavour to the product.
6.3 When the product is packaged in sacks, these must be clean, sturdy, and strongly sewn or sealed.

sections.title: LABELLING
sections.text:
In addition to the requirements of the General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods (CXS 1-1985), the following specific provisions apply:
7.1 The name of the product
The name of the product to be shown on the label shall be “peanuts” or “peanuts in-pod” and type of peanuts.
7.2 Labelling of non-retail containers
Information for non-retail containers shall either be given either on the container or in accompanying documents, except that the name of the product, lot identification and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer shall appear on the container. However, lot identification and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer may be replaced by an identification mark, provided that such a mark is clearly identifiable with the accompanying documents.

sections.title: METHODS OF ANALYSIS AND SAMPLING

sections.title: Damaged Pods: include:
sections.text:
a) shrivelled pods (pods which are imperfectly developed and shrunken); or
b) pods having cracks or broken areas which cause conspicuous openings or which seriously weaken a large portion of the pod, especially if the kernel inside the pod is easily visible without any pressure forced upon the edges of the crack.

sections.title: Discoloured Kernels: kernels are not damaged but are affected by one or more of the following:
path_titles: DISCOLOURED KERNELS: KERNELS ARE NOT DAMAGED BUT ARE AFFECTED BY ONE OR MORE OF THE FOLLOWING:
sections.text:
a) flesh (cotyledon) discolouration which is darker than a light yellow colour or consists of more than a slight yellow pitting of the flesh; and/or
b) skin discolouration which is dark brown, dark grey, dark blue, or black, and covers more than 25% of the kernel.

sentences.original:
Peanuts, either in the pod or in the form of kernels, are obtained from varieties of the species Arachis hypogaea L.

a) those affected by freezing injury causing hard, translucent or discoloured flesh;
b) shrivelled kernels which are imperfectly developed and shrunken; and/or
c) those damaged by insects, worm cuts;
d) mechanical damage;
e) germinated kernels.
1% m/m To be determined
5% m/m
2% m/m
2%m/m
2% m/m

sentences.original:
In addition to the requirements of the General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods (CXS 1-1985), the following specific provisions apply:
7.1 The name of the product
The name of the product to be shown on the label shall be “peanuts” or “peanuts in-pod” and type of peanuts.

pages.title: STANDARD FOR PEANUTS
pages.summary: Página contém título principal do documento 'STANDARD FOR PEANUTS' e cabeçalho 'CXS 200-1995'. Apenas um nó de nível 1 identificado com texto associado sobre adoção e emendas.

Página apresenta estrutura clara com seções numeradas do 1 ao 5 e subseções detalhadas, incluindo níveis de 4 na seção 3.2.3. Texto associado está bem compactado a cada nó. Sem indicações de continuidade entre páginas. Cabeçalho 'CXS 200-1995 2' presente, mas título principal do documento não aparece nesta página.

A página apresenta três seções principais numeradas: 6 PACKAGING, 7 LABELLING e 8 METHODS OF ANALYSIS AND SAMPLING, cada uma com subseções detalhadas de forma clara. Não há indicação de continuidade entre páginas. O cabeçalho contém 'CXS 200-1995 3'. Não há título principal do documento visível nesta página.

A página apresenta um anexo com três seções principais: 1 In-Pod Defects, 2 Kernel Defects e 3 Peanuts other than the designated type. As seções 1 e 2 têm subseções claramente numeradas e descritas. Todo o texto explicativo foi associado ao respectivo título ou subtítulo. Não há indícios de continuidade entre páginas.
